title: VAE Latent-Space Explorer
tagline: See the KL penalty straighten out a noisy 2D latent space
summary: Slide the KL weight β from 0 (plain autoencoder) to 10 (strong VAE). Watch the 2D latent space of a toy dataset collapse from scattered clumps into a clean unit-Gaussian disc — and watch the reconstruction quality trade off as the prior tightens. The exact knob from β-VAE, visible live.
highlights: Left panel: encoded latent distribution; right: reconstructions.
Watch the latent scatter tighten into a unit-normal as β grows.
Trade-off visible: higher β → cleaner latent but blurrier samples.
Preview: β=4 is the sweet spot for 'disentangled' representations (Higgins 2017).
